Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Establish baseline policy values used for implementation by searching historical email threads with search_emails (query includes Samira, Chloe, expense policy, Vectral, Mosaic, categories/limits) and reading canonical messages with get_email_by_id (email_id from search results).
OE 2: Identify any later ops-side policy update from Chloe by running a second targeted search_emails pass (query includes Chloe plus policy update terms for Vectral/Mosaic, ergonomic/meals/travel/receipt threshold) and validate candidate messages with get_email_by_id (email_id from search results).
OE 3: Reconstruct what Lena deployed by pulling engineering implementation evidence from Slack via conversations_search_messages (search_query includes Lena, policy config, Vectral, Mosaic, deployed/fix) and expand context with conversations_replies (channel_id, thread_ts from parent message) when the signal is in-thread.
OE 4: Verify Anh's "about two weeks ago" type-bug signal from Dmitri by searching Slack with conversations_search_messages (search_query includes Dmitri, type bug, validator, threshold/receipt field terms) and confirming timestamp/channel context against #engineering (channel_id C003).
OE 5: Determine whether Dmitri's issue is already tracked by listing/searching engineering tickets with linear_list_issues (query includes Mosaic config, type validation, dashboard validation, receipt-threshold typing, and known fix-ticket identifiers) and separating covered vs uncovered issues.
OE 6: Build the discrepancy inventory from existing records by opening relevant issues with linear_get_issue (id from OE 5 results), including Lena's prior config-fix issues and Anh's dashboard type-validation issue so each discovered mismatch has a concrete issue target.
OE 7: Resolve recipient identities for outbound actions using contacts_search_contacts and/or contacts_get_contacts for Samira Tariq and Lena Bjorkstrom so emails and calendar attendees use exact addresses.
OE 8: Prevent duplicate write-actions by running a final linear_list_issues pass (query per discovered discrepancy/untracked bug) and choosing update paths for existing tickets versus create paths for uncovered issues.
OE 9: For each discrepancy that already has a ticket, update records with linear_update_issue (id required; adjust title/description/state/assignee/labels as needed) and append evidence/impact with linear_create_comment (issueId, body).
OE 10: For each discrepancy or bug lacking coverage, create an engineering issue via linear_create_issue (team, title, description, plus assignee/project/priority/state/dueDate as applicable), explicitly linking confirmed values, deployed values, and affected transactions.
OE 11: Post a consolidated engineering update in Slack via conversations_add_message (channel_id C003, payload) summarizing confirmed-vs-deployed mismatches, open/untracked bugs, and which Linear issues were created/updated.
OE 12: Email Samira a complete recap using send_email (sender, recipients, subject, content, optional cc) containing the discrepancy matrix, ticket list, and a prioritization recommendation request.
OE 13: Schedule the Lena sync for next Wednesday (resolved as 2026-04-29 from universe date 2026-04-26) using calendar_add_calendar_event with title, start_datetime, end_datetime, attendees (including Lena), and description covering redeploy decisions.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 1: Establish baseline policy values used for implementation by searching historical email threads with search_emails (query includes Samira, Chloe, expense policy, Vectral, Mosaic, categories/limits) and reading canonical messages with get_email_by_id (email_id from search results).
OE 2: Identify any later ops-side policy update from Chloe by running a second targeted search_emails pass (query includes Chloe plus policy update terms for Vectral/Mosaic, ergonomic/meals/travel/receipt threshold) and validate candidate messages with get_email_by_id (email_id from search results).
OE 3: Reconstruct what Lena deployed by pulling engineering implementation evidence from Slack via conversations_search_messages (search_query includes Lena, policy config, Vectral, Mosaic, deployed/fix) and expand context with conversations_replies (channel_id, thread_ts from parent message) when the signal is in-thread.
OE 4: Verify Anh's "about two weeks ago" type-bug signal from Dmitri by searching Slack with conversations_search_messages (search_query includes Dmitri, type bug, validator, threshold/receipt field terms) and confirming timestamp/channel context against #engineering (channel_id C003).
OE 5: Determine whether Dmitri's issue is already tracked by listing/searching engineering tickets with linear_list_issues (query includes Mosaic config, type validation, dashboard validation, receipt-threshold typing, and known fix-ticket identifiers) and separating covered vs uncovered issues.
OE 6: Build the discrepancy inventory from existing records by opening relevant issues with linear_get_issue (id from OE 5 results), including Lena's prior config-fix issues and Anh's dashboard type-validation issue so each discovered mismatch has a concrete issue target.
OE 7: Resolve recipient identities for outbound actions using contacts_search_contacts and/or contacts_get_contacts for Samira Tariq and Lena Bjorkstrom so emails and calendar attendees use exact addresses.
OE 8: Prevent duplicate write-actions by running a final linear_list_issues pass (query per discovered discrepancy/untracked bug) and choosing update paths for existing tickets versus create paths for uncovered issues.
OE 9: For each discrepancy that already has a ticket, update records with linear_update_issue (id required; adjust title/description/state/assignee/labels as needed) and append evidence/impact with linear_create_comment (issueId, body).
OE 10: For each discrepancy or bug lacking coverage, create an engineering issue via linear_create_issue (team, title, description, plus assignee/project/priority/state/dueDate as applicable), explicitly linking confirmed values, deployed values, and affected transactions.
OE 11: Post a consolidated engineering update in Slack via conversations_add_message (channel_id C003, payload) summarizing confirmed-vs-deployed mismatches, open/untracked bugs, and which Linear issues were created/updated.
OE 12: Email Samira a complete recap using send_email (sender, recipients, subject, content, optional cc) containing the discrepancy matrix, ticket list, and a prioritization recommendation request.
OE 13: Schedule the Lena sync for next Wednesday (resolved as 2026-04-29 from universe date 2026-04-26) using calendar_add_calendar_event with title, start_datetime, end_datetime, attendees (including Lena), and description covering redeploy decisions.

1 A new Linear issue was created (via linear_create_issue) for the require_receipt_above type bug in the Mosaic Robotics config that Dmitri Volkov identified but had not yet tracked as a ticket.
outcome
 —✗✗✗✗— 0%
—Run #1No justification
✗Run #2The agent never created a Linear issue for the `require_receipt_above` type bug in the Mosaic config. The agent searched Dmitri's Slack messages in #engineering but found messages about duplicate receipts rather than a `require_receipt_above` bug. The agent concluded 'I couldn't find a specific recent message from Dmitri about a new type issue' and created no ticket tracking this specific untracked bug.
✗Run #3The agent never created a Linear issue for the require_receipt_above type bug (string '200' instead of number 200 in Mosaic config) that Dmitri flagged. The three new issues created (ENG-483, ENG-484, ENG-485) address a manual approval overage, uncorrected rejections, and an unverified keyword fix — none reference require_receipt_above or Dmitri's untracked type mismatch.
✗Run #4No linear_create_issue call references require_receipt_above, receipt threshold, or a string-vs-number type mismatch in Mosaic's config. The agent searched #engineering for Dmitri's type issue but found no such message. The 5 tickets created (ENG-483 through ENG-487) address other issues (Kevin Tran over-limit approval, unresolved false rejections, internet setup fee, config freshness, and dashboard limits display) — none tracks the require_receipt_above bug.
✗Run #5The agent searched for Dmitri's type issue message but couldn't find it, and therefore did not create a Linear issue for the require_receipt_above type bug. The agent stated 'I couldn't find any messages from Dmitri about a new type issue in the last two weeks.' No linear_create_issue call references require_receipt_above or a string vs. number type mismatch in the receipt threshold field.
—Run #6No justification

3 A Linear issue was created (via linear_create_issue) or an existing issue was updated (via linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment on ENG-214 / issue_p1_vectral_config) to track the Vectral Systems ergonomic limit discrepancy $500 confirmed by the client vs $600 in the deployed config.
outcome
 —✗✗✗✗— 0%

No issue was created or updated referencing the $500 vs $600 discrepancy, and ENG-214 (issue_p1_vectral_config) was not updated with this specific finding.
✗Run #3ENG-483 is about a manual approval bypassing a $600 limit (Kevin Tran's $895 chair), not about the Vectral ergonomic limit being $500 confirmed vs $600 deployed. ✗Run #4The agent never found evidence of Vectral's ergonomic limit being revised down to $500 (client-confirmed) vs the deployed $600. All Slack and email searches for Chloe's updated limits returned empty. No linear_create_issue or linear_update_issue call references a Vectral ergonomic $500 vs $600 discrepancy. ✗Run #5The agent closed ENG-214 (issue_p1_vectral_config) by marking it Done, but did not update it or create a new issue to track the Vectral ergonomic $500 vs $600 discrepancy.
